Class GameLogic

java.lang.Object
com.example.elcincuentazo.model.GameLogic
public class GameLogic extends Object
Central game-logic engine for Cincuentazo.

This class orchestrates every phase of the game:

  1. Setup (setupGame(int)) – creates the deck, creates all players, deals 4 cards each, places the initial table card.
  2. Turn execution (humanPlayCard(Card)) – validates and executes a card play by the human player, then draws a replacement card.
  3. Elimination checking (checkAndEliminateCurrentPlayer()) – detects when a player has no legal move.
  4. Win detection (isGameOver()) – declares a winner when only one player remains active.

Machine turns are initiated by TurnManager, which calls executeMachinePlay() from a background thread after an appropriate delay.

The game fires events to registered GameEventListeners so that the View can update the GUI without coupling to the Model.

See Also:
  • Deck
  • Player
  • TurnManager

  • Constructor Details

    • GameLogic

      public GameLogic()
      Constructs a new GameLogic instance. Call setupGame(int) before starting to play.

  • Method Details

    • addGameEventListener

      public void addGameEventListener(GameEventListener listener)
      Registers a GameEventListener to receive game-event callbacks.
      Parameters:
      listener - the listener to add; must not be null

    • removeGameEventListener

      public void removeGameEventListener(GameEventListener listener)
      Removes a previously registered GameEventListener.
      Parameters:
      listener - the listener to remove

    • setupGame

      public void setupGame(int machineCount)
      Sets up a new game with the given number of machine opponents.

      Steps performed:

      1. Creates a fresh, shuffled Deck.
      2. Creates one HumanPlayer and machineCount MachinePlayers.
      3. Deals 4 cards to each player from the draw pile.
      4. Places one card face-up on the table to start the sum.
      5. Fires onGameStarted on all listeners.
      Parameters:
      machineCount - number of machine players to create (1, 2, or 3)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if machineCount is not 1, 2, or 3

    • humanPlayCard

      public void humanPlayCard(Card card) throws InvalidCardPlayException, GameOverException
      Executes a card play by the human player.

      The Controller calls this method in response to the player clicking a card in the GUI. The method:

      1. Validates the play (sum must not exceed 50).
      2. Removes the card from the human's hand and places it on the table.
      3. Updates the table sum.
      4. Draws a replacement card from the deck.
      5. Advances the turn to the next active player.
      Parameters:
      card - the card the human wishes to play
      Throws:
      InvalidCardPlayException - if the card would push the sum above 50
      GameOverException - if the game has already ended
      IllegalStateException - if it is not currently the human's turn

    • executeMachinePlay

      public void executeMachinePlay() throws GameOverException
      Executes the current machine player's full turn atomically.

      TurnManager calls this method after the appropriate "thinking" delay. The method:

      1. Verifies it is actually a machine's turn.
      2. Asks the machine to choose the best playable card.
      3. If no playable card exists, eliminates the machine.
      4. Otherwise plays the chosen card, updates the sum, draws a replacement, and advances the turn.

      All listener callbacks issued here will be called on whatever thread this method runs on. TurnManager is responsible for dispatching those calls back to the JavaFX Application Thread via Platform.runLater before calling this method.

      Throws:
      GameOverException - if the game has already ended
      IllegalStateException - if the current player is actually human

    • checkAndEliminateCurrentPlayer

      public boolean checkAndEliminateCurrentPlayer()
      Checks whether the current player must be eliminated (no playable card) and, if so, triggers elimination.

      The Controller should call this at the start of each human turn to detect an edge case where the human cannot play any card after an opponent has raised the sum.

      Returns:
      true if the current player was eliminated

    • isGameOver

      public boolean isGameOver()
      Returns true if the game has ended (only one active player remains or the game-over flag has been set).
      Returns:
      true when the game is over

    • getWinner

      public Player getWinner()
      Returns the winning player, or null if the game is not yet over.
      Returns:
      winner or null

    • getTableSum

      public int getTableSum()
      Returns the current accumulated sum on the table.
      Returns:
      table sum

    • getCurrentPlayer

      public Player getCurrentPlayer()
      Returns the player who is currently taking their turn.
      Returns:
      current Player, or null if game is over

    • getPlayers

      public List<Player> getPlayers()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of all players (active and eliminated), in the turn order established at setup.
      Returns:
      player list

    • getActivePlayers

      public List<Player> getActivePlayers()
      Returns only the players who are still active.
      Returns:
      list of active players

    • getGameState

      public GameState getGameState()
      Returns a snapshot of the current game state suitable for passing to the View layer without exposing mutable internals.
      Returns:
      current GameState snapshot

    • getDeck

      public Deck getDeck()
      Returns the Deck used in this game. Exposed for unit testing; the View and Controller should not modify it directly.
      Returns:
      the game's deck
